summary: McKenzie reacts negatively to Google Analytics' redesigned interface, finding it less usable than the previous version. The new widgetized reporting format buries information that was previously accessible through straightforward drill-downs. He specifically struggles to answer basic business questions like month-over-month conversion rate comparisons. He hopes to eventually master the new interface but is frustrated by the productivity loss in the meantime.
key_points: Google Analytics' redesigned interface prioritizes a trendy Web 2.0 widgetized format over practical usability
Key business metrics like conversion rate comparisons became harder to access after the redesign
Redesigns that force users to relearn workflows impose real productivity costs on businesses
money_quotes: Information which was previously available through drilling down is apparently being forced into a new widgetized reporting format, which might be great but seems like it has a learning curve steeper than Mt. Everest.

Getting to the information which is of paramount interest to me, like "Am I doing better this month than last month on Google, in terms of conversion rates?" seems to be much more difficult than it used to be.
key_insight: A product redesign that makes core workflows harder to accomplish is a step backward, regardless of how modern the new interface looks.
